databases

analytics

On this page

analytics — client.analytics ​

Emit custom analytics events and control the analytics pipeline.

Divergent shape

Swift flattens the analytics surface onto the client (client.logAnalyticsEvent(...), client.flushAnalytics()) and takes an untyped [String: Any] where JS uses the typed client.analytics.logEvent({ ... }). Both compile; the shapes differ (#954, #963).

logEvent(event) ​

Emit a custom analytics event. action and user_ulid are required.

ts
client.analytics.logEvent({
  action: "photo_uploaded",
  feature: "gallery",
  user_ulid: userUlid,
});
swift
client.logAnalyticsEvent([
  "action": "photo_uploaded",
  "feature": "gallery",
  "user_ulid": userUlid,
])

flush() ​

Force-flush the buffered analytics queue immediately.

ts
client.analytics.flush();
swift
client.flushAnalytics()

logSnapshot(context?) ​

Emit a snapshot event with arbitrary context.

No Swift equivalent

JavaScript-only — the Swift client has no logSnapshot (#963).

ts
client.analytics.logSnapshot({ screen: "settings", theme: "dark" });
